mapping.a_element: transition (T) operator describing scattering off each body
mapping.b_element: per-bond/vertex scattering amplitude (unitary S-matrix element) of the quantized graph

mapping.a_element: free Green's function G0 propagating the field between scatterers
mapping.b_element: free propagation phase exp(ikL) along a bond of length L

mapping.a_element: round-trip operator T_a G0 T_b G0 built from T-operators and propagators
mapping.b_element: bond-scattering evolution operator S(k) that maps directed bonds to directed bonds

mapping.a_element: Casimir energy written as Tr ln(1 - T_a G0 T_b G0) (Kenneth-Klich scattering formula)
mapping.b_element: secular determinant det(1 - S(k)) whose ln-det/trace generates the quantum spectrum

mapping.a_element: multiple-scattering expansion: Tr ln(1-.) as a sum over closed paths bouncing between the bodies
mapping.b_element: periodic-orbit trace formula: the same geometric expansion as a sum over periodic orbits on the graph
density of states / quantum spectrum being computed

brief_justification: Both are the same scattering-determinant machinery: a spectral quantity is written as Tr ln(1 - [scattering operator]) — a T-operator round-trip T_a G0 T_b G0 for the Casimir energy (A) versus the bond S-matrix det(1 - S(k)) for the graph spectrum (B) — and the log-det is expanded as a geometric series whose n-th term sums over closed multiple-scattering paths (A) = periodic orbits (B). The T-operators map role-for-role onto the bond scattering amplitudes and the free Green's function onto the bond propagation phase, so the trace-formula / multiple-scattering expansion is the shared underlying method.
